title: Verify and Cite Practical Claims
summary: Evaluate practical claims by checking source quality, recency, evidence, and applicability before acting.
plain_language: Before you trust a claim, check who said it, when they said it, what evidence they show, and whether it fits your situation.

title: Measurement and Units for Practical Work
summary: Understand quantities, conversions, estimation, and tolerances so plans and calculations use comparable units.
plain_language: If you cannot measure it in the same units, you cannot compare it or size it correctly.

title: Map Household and Community Systems
summary: Map inputs, outputs, dependencies, and failure points in a system so you can see what matters first.
plain_language: Draw the system before you try to fix the system.

title: Use AI for Grounded Research and Synthesis
summary: Use AI to summarize, compare, localize, and draft while keeping claims tied to checkable sources and local constraints.
plain_language: Use AI like a fast research assistant and translator, not like a prophet.

title: Build a Household Resilience Inventory
summary: Create a baseline inventory of needs, dependencies, tools, contacts, and vulnerabilities for a household.
plain_language: Write down what the household depends on before an outage or disruption writes it down for you.

title: Store Potable Water Safely
summary: Store potable water in usable quantities, containers, and intervals so it stays available and identifiable.
plain_language: Clean water only helps if you can store enough of it, find it fast, and trust the container.

title: Select an Appropriate Water Treatment Method
summary: Understand treatment methods and match them to the likely contaminants, water source, and available equipment.
plain_language: Different water problems need different fixes; no single treatment method solves everything.

title: Run Basic Household Water Checks and Tests
summary: Inspect and test source or treated water at a household level and record the results in a simple log.
plain_language: A small amount of testing catches many obvious failures before they become a household problem.

title: Build a 72-Hour and 2-Week Household Water Plan
summary: Produce a practical household water plan covering quantity, storage, treatment, testing, and contingency options.
plain_language: A water plan turns scattered gear and half-remembered ideas into a usable operating system.

title: Design a Functional Household Pantry
summary: Design a pantry around calories, diet, shelf life, cooking needs, budget, and storage conditions.
plain_language: A pantry works when it matches what people actually eat, how they cook, and how long they may need it.

title: Rotate and Replenish Pantry Stock
summary: Rotate and replenish stored food systematically so stock remains familiar, usable, and current.
plain_language: Stored food is only useful if it stays edible, visible, and part of normal household practice.

title: Understand Safe Food Preservation Boundaries
summary: Understand the safe boundaries of freezing, drying, fermenting, and canning-related decisions before preserving food.
plain_language: Preservation methods create very different risks; do not treat them as interchangeable.

title: Start Seeds Reliably
summary: Start plants from seed with correct timing, medium, light, moisture, and labeling.
plain_language: Strong seedlings come from consistent conditions, not from luck.

title: Plan a Beginner Garden System
summary: Plan a small bed or container garden for reliable output, maintenance, and learning rather than maximal complexity.
plain_language: A small garden that fits your time and space teaches more than a sprawling one you cannot maintain.

title: Understand the Building Envelope
summary: Understand how heat, air, moisture, insulation, and leakage pathways affect comfort, cost, and damage.
plain_language: The envelope is the boundary between indoors and outdoors; when it leaks air or traps moisture badly, the whole house pays for it.

title: Run a Basic Weatherization Audit
summary: Inspect drafts, heat loss, leakage, and weatherization priorities to rank practical improvements.
plain_language: A weatherization audit tells you where comfort and energy losses are actually happening instead of where you guess they are.

title: Control Moisture and Interrupt Mold Conditions
summary: Identify moisture sources and interrupt the conditions that allow mold and building damage to persist.
plain_language: Mold control starts with moisture control; cleaning alone is not a strategy if the source remains.

title: Use Essential Hand Tools Confidently
summary: Use measuring, tightening, cutting, fastening, and basic repair tools safely and effectively.
plain_language: Tool literacy lowers the barrier to fixing simple problems before they become expensive ones.

title: Locate Shutoffs, Seal Gaps, and Make Minor Temporary Repairs
summary: Locate shutoffs, stop minor leaks, seal gaps, and make temporary repairs that buy time and reduce damage.
plain_language: Fast, basic intervention often matters more than elegant intervention in the first minutes of a household problem.

title: Understand Basic Circuits and Household Loads
summary: Understand volts, amps, watts, watt-hours, breakers, and common household loads so power choices are realistic.
plain_language: Power problems become much easier once you know the difference between how fast energy is used and how much energy you have stored.

title: Run a Critical Loads Audit
summary: Inventory critical devices and outage priorities so backup plans are sized for reality rather than guesswork.
plain_language: You do not need to power everything; you need to know what truly matters and what it costs to keep it running.

title: Estimate Runtime from Stored or Generated Power
summary: Calculate runtime from batteries, generators, or power stations using load, efficiency, and duty-cycle assumptions.
plain_language: Runtime is where wishful thinking meets arithmetic.

title: Draft a Tiered Backup Power Plan
summary: Produce low-, mid-, and high-budget backup options tied to critical loads, runtime goals, and local constraints.
plain_language: A good backup plan gives you options instead of one expensive fantasy setup.

title: Map Local Skills, Tools, Sources, and Shared Assets
summary: Map local tools, skills, food sources, water sources, spaces, and contacts that matter in normal times and disruptions.
plain_language: Community resilience improves when people know what already exists nearby and who can do what.

title: Deliver a Teach-Forward Session
summary: Teach one completed capability to others in a 10–20 minute session using a durable artifact and clear language.
plain_language: Knowledge spreads when someone can pass it on simply, not when they merely possess it privately.
